question.text: An 'Assessee' as defined under Section 2(7) of the Income Tax Act, 1961 includes:

answer.text: Only individuals who have filed their income tax return
Only persons whose income exceeds the basic exemption limit
Every person who is liable to pay any tax, interest, penalty, or any other sum under the Act
Only persons who have been issued an assessment order by the Assessing Officer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 2(7) defines 'Assessee' broadly as a person by whom any tax or any other sum of money (interest, penalty, etc.) is payable under the Act. It also includes every person in respect of whom any proceeding has been taken, or who is deemed to be an assessee or assessee in default under any provision. **The Trap:** Option (a) limits it to return-filers — but even non-filers liable to pay tax are assessees. Option (b) incorrectly ties it to exemption limits — a person could be an assessee in default regardless of income level. Option (d) requires an assessment order — but a person is an assessee by virtue of liability, not by formal assessment. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers this definition in the context of various entity types being taxed. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The broad definition of 'assessee' means even someone liable to deduct TDS on behalf of others can be deemed an assessee — investment advisers must understand this when advising on TDS compliance.
